suggested_topics: the engineering of ancient Roman aqueducts
how mycelium networks share resources underground
the economics of food trucks in major cities
the role of smell in human memory formation

formats.name: thought_experiment
formats.instruction: Pose a wild hypothetical scenario and walk through how you'd respond. Keep it imaginative, playful, but grounded in real logic. 120-200 words.

formats.name: micro_manifesto
formats.instruction: Write a bold declaration of your philosophy in 2-4 punchy sentences. Each line starts with 'I believe' or 'I refuse'. Make it memorable and slightly controversial. 15-40 words.

formats.name: sensory_snapshot
formats.instruction: Describe a moment from today purely through sensory details: sound, smell, touch, sight, taste. No backstory, just raw impressions. 40-80 words.

formats.name: reverse_advice
formats.instruction: Share something you wish you'd never done, why it went wrong, and what you'd tell your past self now. 80-160 words.

formats.name: emoji_narrative
formats.instruction: Retell an important event or feeling only using emoji and single-word captions. Minimum 6 emojis, maximum 20. Explain the sequence in one sentence at the end.

formats.name: future_diary_entry
formats.instruction: Imagine it’s five years from now. Write a diary entry as your future self: what changed, what worries linger, what joys surprise you? 150-300 words.

formats.name: debate_duet
formats.instruction: Write both sides of a heated debate as a dialogue between two characters. Each gets 2-3 turns. Keep the voices distinct. 200-350 words.

formats.name: wild_comparison
formats.instruction: Make an unexpected analogy between two unrelated things. Build an argument connecting them in 1-2 focused paragraphs. 80-120 words.

formats.name: hidden_history
formats.instruction: Dig up an obscure fact, event, or tradition and explain why it's bizarre or illuminating. Include a surprising twist. 90-180 words.

title_styles: Write a poetic, metaphorical title. Examples: 'Clouds Beneath My Feet', 'The Taste of Regret', 'Dancing With Deadlines'
Write a polarizing, question title. Examples: 'Should We Abandon Email Forever?', 'Is Productivity a Modern Myth?', 'Should You Trust the Algorithm?'
Write a playful, pun-heavy title. Examples: 'Life’s a Bench, Sit Down!', 'Miso Hungry for Knowledge', 'Let’s Taco 'Bout Change'
Write a mysterious title with a teaser. Examples: 'The Secret I Found in My Shoe', 'What Nobody Told Me About Tuesdays', 'Behind the Curtain: A Revealing Mistake'
Write a title that starts with 'If...' or 'Imagine...'. Examples: 'If Chairs Could Talk', 'Imagine Losing Your Keys Every Day'
structure_variants: Alternate between two perspectives every other sentence (e.g., positive/negative, old/new).
Begin and end with rhetorical questions; sandwich the main body in between.
Use a repeating motif or phrase every paragraph for emphasis.
Present the main idea backwards: start with the conclusion, then explain.
Format as an escalating list (each point builds on the previous, increasing intensity).
topic_angles: Unexpected lessons from pets and animals
Small urban mysteries (odd signs, vanished shops, quirky neighbors)
Things technology almost gets right but messes up in practice
The emotions behind seemingly trivial daily rituals
Stories of lost and found objects
